messages.text: The next morning, I slammed my head onto my desk and clawed at my hair in pure regret. I didn't care about the stares.
Why did I reject her? Am I an idiot? I'd repeated those questions all night without sleeping.
Calm down. I can still fix this. Just reapply to the club. Humiliating, sure... but I can still rescue the situation.
Just then, the classroom buzzed. Tendou-san stood at the door, smiling elegantly as always.

messages.name: Karen
messages.text: Good morning, Amano-kun. What are you up to?

messages.name: Keita
messages.text: Oh... just a mobile game...

messages.name: Karen
messages.text: Huh. Didn't expect you to like something so boring.
She ignored my phone and leaned in close — closer than yesterday.

messages.name: Karen
messages.text: Anyway. Please reconsider. I have real passion for this club, and I'm very interested in you.
She quietly extended her right hand. She looked like the Buddha lowering a spider thread to save a sinner.
I switched my phone to my left hand and slowly reached out my right—
MONO rescue request: Five seconds remaining.
Instead of taking her hand, my thumb tapped Accept Rescue.
I had neglected her outstretched hand. A mobile game had taken priority over a real person reaching out.
Guilt hit me immediately.

messages.name: Karen
messages.text: Amano-kun... is that boring mobile game more important than my invitation?
I lifted my head and smiled. For once, I didn't stutter.

messages.name: Keita
messages.text: Tendou-san. Some things that seem boring to you hold meaning for me.

messages.name: Keita
messages.text: So I'm sorry, but I still won't join. That club doesn't have the game I want to play.

messages.name: Keita
messages.text: I respect everyone there. Working hard to improve — that's a great way to enjoy games.

messages.name: Keita
messages.text: But I prefer just having fun. Playing happily, without competing against anyone.
I thought about my brother. We share almost nothing. But when we play games, we laugh like idiots — even on terrible school days.

messages.name: Keita
messages.text: Games are my refuge. My comfort. They're how a hopeless guy like me connects with people.

messages.name: Keita
messages.text: That's why I want games to stay as pure entertainment — something that can save people.

messages.name: Keita
messages.text: So I'm sorry. The club is amazing. But my resolve is firm — I'll keep playing my way.
Tendou-san's gaze dropped. Her whole body trembled.

messages.name: Karen
messages.text: Why does this feel like I'm being rejected...? I never imagined someone like you would reject me...
She slammed my desk, face red with tears. Then she spun around and stormed out.
I guess... I picked the wrong dialogue option.
After a while, I cleared MONO's rescue quest before the bell rang.
Then my phone buzzed. A message from MONO. I opened it to find one short sentence.
I read those words four times. A wave of relief and gratitude washed over me.
I looked out the window. No pretty girls, no drama. Just my ordinary, half-baked daily life... but it wasn't all bad.
Today, once again, games walked beside me through my ridiculously ordinary day.
